description_long: This object is a preserved crab with a broad, oval-shaped carapace that is mottled in shades of gray and brown, giving it a weathered, natural appearance. The crab features two prominent, asymmetrical claws, with one noticeably larger than the other, and several spindly walking legs extending from its sides. The carapace is textured and slightly rough, with subtle ridges and a pale central stripe running from front to back. The underside is lighter in color, with visible segmentation and jointed appendages. The eyes are small and positioned on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace. No antennae are clearly visible, and the overall size is compact, comparable to a small fist.
description: A small, gray-brown crab with a broad, textured carapace, two large claws, and multiple spindly legs. The surface is rough and mottled, with a pale stripe and small eyes on short stalks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad carapace, both claws (one larger), and the pale central stripe.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the carapace, the large claw, and the layered legs beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the segmented underside, walking legs, and the back of the carapace.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the smaller claw and the curvature of the body and legs.
